spec_match.rationale: The whole simulator inverts the spec change: it claims pre-v137 was NotAllowedError and v137+ is SecurityError, but the actual rename (per chromestatus 5160752715137024 and SPC PR #281) is the opposite — SecurityError -> NotAllowedError. Every scenario panel and the comparison grid teach the wrong migration.

interactive.rationale: Real controls — scenario select, run/reset buttons, clickable step cards driving a detail box and a regenerating comparison grid.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index has TWO paragraphs for this concept — one accurate-ish, one duplicating the simulator pitch — and the second already trips the inverted-direction problem (`SecurityError vs the old NotAllowedError`). Tighten to one blurb that matches the spec direction.

edge_cases.rationale: Four scenarios cover the headline paths but they conflate three independent conditions (activation, RP-origin, permissions-policy) under one bogus rename. Only the no-activation cross-origin path is actually affected — the others are unchanged.

references.rationale: Page has no `references` or `see also` block at all — no chromestatus link, no spec link. CLAUDE.md requires every concept page cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> URL for the slug self-heal recovery.

design_system.rationale: Mostly variables, but the inline `button { background: var(--text-black); color: var(--bg-ivory); }` override duplicates the global rule and then `.secondary` inverts both — fine. However, `color-mix(... var(--accent-amber) 70%, var(--bg-paper))` is fine. The `tick`/`cross` emoji (✓ / ✗) inside step indicators conflict with the no-emoji rule in feedback_svg_icons memory: use inline SVG instead.

openQuestions.title: Reverse the entire pre/post mapping
openQuestions.detail: Every SCENARIOS step and every COMPARISONS row says pre-v137 was NotAllowedError and v137+ is SecurityError. The real change is SecurityError -> NotAllowedError. Flip the direction of `pre` and `post` in COMPARISONS, rewrite the scenario `detail` strings (search for `SecurityError is thrown (Chrome 137+)` and swap), and update the lede / `Error alignment` blurb. This is a content-truth bug, not cosmetic — anyone copying the migration matrix will write code that ships broken in 137+.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus + spec references
openQuestions.detail: Add a `references` section with the chromestatus.com/feature/5160752715137024 link and the secure-payment-confirmation PR #281 spec link. Without the chromestatus link the slug self-heal script (/tmp/fix-slugs.py) cannot recover this folder.

openQuestions.title: Drop the `wrong-origin` and `no-permission` scenarios
openQuestions.detail: Those paths were already NotAllowedError before v137 and remain so — they are not part of the rename. Either remove them or relabel them as `unaffected` so the simulator does not imply the rename touches them. Keep `no-user-gesture` (the actual affected path, in a cross-origin iframe) and `success`.

openQuestions.title: Replace tick/cross emoji with inline SVG icons
openQuestions.detail: Step indicators currently use ✓ / ✗ glyphs in template strings. Per project memory (feedback_svg_icons), all UI status indicators should be inline SVG. Swap to a small <svg> check / cross sourced from the design system.

summary: Strongest interactive UX in the feature, but it teaches the rename backwards — every comparison row and scenario detail string has pre and post swapped. Also no references block. Two truth-level bugs; the UX scaffold is reusable once they are fixed.
